name: Taco Oyster Crackers
directions: Put all ingredients in a large tupperware bowl and shake well.
Keep covered until ready to eat!

name: Hidden Valley Oyster Crackers
directions: Combine ranch mix and oil with a whisk.
Add the garlic powder.
Pour over crackers in a large bowl.
Mix well to cover.
Place crackers on a baking sheet and put in a warm oven for 15-20 minutes.

name: Grandma's Oyster Crackers
directions: Mix oil, dressing mix, lemon pepper seasoning, dill, and garlic salt.
Pour over 2 (10 oz) packages oyster crackers.
Serve and enjoy!

name: Dill and Garlic Oyster Crackers
directions: Mix all ingredients in bowl.
shake occassionally.

name: Oyster Cracker Snacks
directions: Place crackers in large bowl.
Mix all other ingredients in a seperate bowl.
Drizzle over crackers, stirring gently to mix thoroughly.
Place in ziplock bag.
Can substitute 1 tsp dill in place of Italian Powder and onion powder.

name: Holiday Oyster Cracker Snacks
directions: Mix all ingredients together.
Allow to sit one hour for flavors to blend.

name: No-Bake-Just-Shake Cracker Mix
directions: In a small bowl, combine ranch dressing mix, oil, dill and garlic powder. Mix well.
In large bowl, combine oyster crackers, cheese crackers and pretzels. Pour oil mixture over crackers and toss well until coated. Cover.
Toss mixture every 30 minutes for 2 hours. Store in air-tight container.

name: Ritz Snacker Crackers
directions: Preheat oven to 250 degrees F.
Mix crackers together in a pan or baking dish that has been sprayed with Pam.
Mix the rest of the ingredients together and pour over the crackers, stirring to coat evenly.
Bake in oven for 15 minutes, stirring every 5 minutes.
Allow to cool and enjoy.

name: Zesty Crackers
directions: Empty the Ritz crackers out of the box into a gallon ziplock bag.
Pour 1/2 cup of oil into the bag.
Mix lightly being careful not to break the crackers.
Meanwhile mix your ranch packet, garlic powder, dill, and pepper flakes (optional) in a bowl.
Once crackers are fully coated in oil mix in 1/2 of the ranch mixture.
Mix for a couple minutes.
Pour in the last of the ranch mixture.
Continue to mix untill coated in ranch mixture.
The bag will look a little grimey so transfer thr bag of crackers into the clean bag.
